column3: Action
column4: Expected Result

column3: Log in with your assigned credentials.
column4: Dashboard loads showing vendor statistics (Total Vendors, Active, Pending Review), a Procurement row (Open RFQs, Bids to Review, PRs, Comparisons), Recent Registrations, and Recent RFQs.

column3: Read the dashboard cards and counters.
column4: All numbers render correctly with no blanks, "undefined", or error messages.

column3: Open the Vendors page from the sidebar.
column4: A paginated vendor list loads with search and filters (status, type, category, city).

column3: Search for a vendor name and/or filter by category.
column4: The list narrows to show only matching vendors.

column3: Click on a vendor to open their profile.
column4: Profile, Documents, and History tabs all load without errors.

column3: Use Flag Duplicate and Blacklist on a test vendor.
column4: Blacklist asks for confirmation before proceeding; the vendor's status updates accordingly. Use Restore to reverse a blacklist.

column3: Use Invite Vendor: enter a name, an email address you control, and a category.
column4: A confirmation is shown on screen; an invitation email arrives at the inbox you specified.

column3: Open the public Registration form in a separate browser window and submit it with sample documents.
column4: Submission succeeds and a thank-you confirmation is displayed.

column3: Return to the platform and open Pending Registrations.
column4: The newly registered vendor appears in the queue with a document view.

column3: Click Approve on the pending vendor.
column4: The vendor moves to Listed status and disappears from the pending queue.

column3: Open the Categories page.
column4: Vendor categories mapped to procurement groups are displayed; the mapping is viewable.

column3: Open the PR Tracker page.
column4: The purchase-requisition view renders; the count matches the PRs number on the Dashboard.

column3: Start a New RFQ and upload a small Materials SAP Excel file.
column4: The file parses successfully; the platform shows active vs already-procured item counts.

column3: Click Generate.
column4: Draft RFQ(s) are created, one per category, and appear on the RFQ Tracker.

column3: Open a draft RFQ.
column4: Overview, Vendors, and Bids tabs are shown; items are listed; the Vendors tab lists matched vendors.

column3: On the Vendors tab, remove the auto-suggested real vendors and add an 'SCC TEST' vendor. Set a deadline.
column4: Real vendors are removed; the test vendor is added; the deadline is saved.

column3: Review and edit the RFQ email, then click Send.
column4: A Drive folder is created; the email arrives at the test inbox; the RFQ status changes to Issued; the vendor row shows a sent time.

column3: From the test inbox, reply to the RFQ email with item prices, payment terms, VAT, lead time, and validity.
column4: The reply is sent successfully from the test inbox.

column3: Wait up to approximately 15 minutes, then check the RFQ.
column4: A new bid appears for the RFQ marked "pending review"; the Dashboard "Bids to Review" count increases.

column3: Open Bid Review (two-panel view).
column4: The left panel shows the original email; the right panel shows the AI-extracted prices and terms with confidence colours (green, amber, or blank).

column3: Correct any extraction errors and click Confirm Bid.
column4: The bid status becomes confirmed and feeds into the comparison.

column3: Repeat steps 5.4–5.9 with a second SCC TEST vendor to obtain two confirmed bids.
column4: A second confirmed bid is now available for comparison.

column3: Open the Comparison page for the RFQ.
column4: A per-item comparison table is shown with the lowest price highlighted; a per-vendor commercial summary displays subtotal, VAT, total, payment terms, and lead time.

column3: Click Generate Recommendation.
column4: A written recommendation appears with reasoning, a payment-terms note, caveats, and an alternative vendor.

column3: Mark the RFQ as final and click Export.
column4: An Excel comparison sheet downloads to your computer.

column3: Open the Outreach page.
column4: Campaign stats are shown (all currently zero) along with campaign history. The "Start Campaign" button is disabled with an explanatory tooltip. Do not attempt to enable it.

column3: Open Settings > Theme and switch between Emerald, Crimson, and Charcoal.
column4: The entire application re-themes with the selected colour scheme; the choice persists after a page reload.
